Trump has said the issue of the Arctic as a choice between Greenland and NATO. For the White House, it’s a two-for-one: Take Greenland and lose NATO. I don't think Americans fully understand how much harm this would cause our national security. https://t.co/iZTSUhIUEO

Here's the full text of my op ed on Trump's destructive decision to impose tariffs on key European allies over Greenland. @Telegraph

Under the 1951 US-Danish “Defense of Greenland Treaty,” the U.S. can build military facilities on Greenland. Danish officials have made it clear they will approve new facilities. The kind of cooperation we saw in WW2 and the Cold War is possible again.

https://t.co/SoYsSWff03

I have long said that a secure Greenland is undoubtedly critical for the West. But Trump’s latest bizarre outburst threatening tariffs on our allies in return for Greenland makes security and stability in the Arctic much harder to accomplish.

Donald Trump’s tariffs against the U.K. and other allies for their position regarding Greenland is without a doubt his most reckless assertion as President to date.

https://t.co/SoYsSWff03

The Iranian regime is desperately afraid that the United States is about to strike... They saw us grab Maduro. They should be worried. The regime is very weak, but the U.S. needs to have a policy that says our objective is regime change. https://t.co/MfDrMw2apS

The U.S. has the coercive power to help Venezuela dump the entire Maduro regime, but we won't for much longer. If the White House had acted against the entire regime while grabbing Maduro, things would be in a much different position now. https://t.co/L3k1iInNmB

There is a huge risk of two alternative governments being set up in Venezuela, as we saw in 2018 and 2019, and losing the opportunity to oust not only Maduro, but the whole regime. https://t.co/W1N0C3bPpH

The U.S. does not need to put boots on the ground in order to help the opposition in Iran. There are intelligence and communications resources we can offer at this time.
